name: Ethel Waters
description: American blues, jazz and gospel vocalist and actress (1896-1977)
abstract: Ethel Waters (October 31, 1896 – September 1, 1977) was an American singer and actress. Waters frequently performed jazz, swing, and pop music on the Broadway stage and in concerts.

concerts.label: Ethel Waters and the Hall Johnson Choir

works.t: I Ain't Gonna Sin No More
I Can't Stay Here by Myself

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: He said... she is suddenly in a class with Ethel Waters, Fanny Brice, and Peggy Lee.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] He's got Ethel Waters. He had her for a long time.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: I'm trying to think of that guy that danced. He had his wife and she was a singer and that's the first show I arranged for him at the Lafayette Theatre, that's in '28 and then I arranged for Ethel Waters.

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur "Monk" Hazel
about_quotes.evidence: I wound up going with the mellophone, leaving, finishing up our date, and then going over there and making, making the, making the date behind, behind Ethel Waters with them.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: we traveled on the road with Ethel Waters... And we did that tour with Ethel Waters... we played with Ethel Waters at TOBA.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: I played for her so many times. Bessie was something else. I mean Ethel.
